destinations.evidence: The registry reader returns committed input_hash and previous-block/executor context without dynamic full input bytes.
Builder settlement verification obtains full input from settlement/pool calldata and checks it against the committed hash before SPC signature verification.

equivalence_argument: The port stores/reads only the committed input hash from AsyncJobTracker for SPC jobs; the full bytes come from settlement calldata or pool metadata and are hash-checked before verification.
notes: Mapped in async registry reader and settlement verification.
rationale: The primary diff replaces `SpcVerificationJobData.precompile_input` with `input_hash`, removes the old dynamic byte reader, and documents that the caller extracts full bytes from settlement calldata for verification. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: SPC verification job reads must return the committed precompile input hash plus previous-block context and executor metadata, while the full input bytes are expected to come from settlement transaction calldata.
suggested_falsification_tests: Populate mock storage for an SPC job with previous block fields and an input hash but without dynamic input bytes, call `read_jobs_for_spc_verification`, and assert the result contains `input_hash` and no full input bytes; verify missing executor storage still skips the job.
summary: SPC job reads use committed input hashes only.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Populate AsyncJobTracker storage with input_hash and previous-block fields but no dynamic input bytes, then assert read_jobs_for_spc_verification returns input_hash and verifier rejects mismatched calldata bytes.
title: SPC reads committed input hash only
